title: On Lagrangian Quasi-Cobordisms
speaker: Angela Wu
abstract: A Lagrangian cobordism between Legendrian knots is an important notion in symplectic geometry. Many questions, including basic structural questions about these surfaces are yet unanswered. For instance, while it is known that these cobordisms form a preorder, and that they are not symmetric, it is not known if they form a partial order on Legendrian knots. The idea of a Lagrangian quasi-cobordism was first defined by Sabloff, Vela-Vick, and Wong.

proof_stages.label: Exact Lagrangian cobordism background
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls exact Lagrangian cobordisms between Legendrian links in standard contact R^3, including cylindrical ends, exactness with constant primitive, concordance, genus minimization, preorder behavior, and classical/DGA obstructions.

proof_stages.label: Decomposable moves and common bounds
proof_stages.summary: Decomposable Lagrangian cobordisms are built from Legendrian isotopy, births, and pinches, and known results give common lower and upper Lagrangian bounds for Legendrian knots with the same rotation number.

proof_stages.label: Lagrangian joins and quasi-cobordisms
proof_stages.summary: A Lagrangian join is the upper-bound zigzag between two same-rotation Legendrians, and a Lagrangian quasi-cobordism is a connected sequence of such joins, making quasi-concordance an equivalence relation rather than the nonsymmetric cobordism relation.

proof_stages.label: Strict separation from Lagrangian concordance
proof_stages.summary: The speaker proves that quasi-concordance is strictly weaker than Lagrangian concordance by choosing two Lagrangian-slice knots, applying a satellite-concordance theorem, and using ruling-polynomial inequalities for their Whitehead doubles to obstruct concordance in either direction.

proof_stages.label: Minimal-genus metric monoid
proof_stages.summary: The minimal genus over all quasi-cobordisms between same-rotation Legendrians defines a metric on quasi-concordance classes, while connected sum gives the monoid operation.

proof_stages.label: Unboundedness and infinite valence
proof_stages.summary: Using genus-minimizing Lagrangian cobordisms and Alexander-polynomial obstructions, the talk proves that every class has representatives arbitrarily far away and infinitely many pairwise distinct classes at distance one.

proof_stages.label: Further metric and satellite properties
proof_stages.summary: The final discussion states additional metric-monoid phenomena and open problems, including failures of some smooth amphichiral behavior, bounded-distance satellite operators, winding-number-one satellites as quasi-isometries, and obstacles to adapting smooth ribbon-concordance arguments.

examples.name: Disconnected birth-plus-pinch double-stabilization pathology
examples.note: The speaker shows that allowing disconnected pieces lets a birth followed by a pinch double-stabilize a Legendrian without changing class, forcing the connectedness condition in the definition.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker explicitly runs the disconnected birth move followed by a pinch, tracing how it produces a double stabilization and why that pathology must be excluded.

passes.reason: The disconnected cylinder-plus-birth followed by a pinch uses a trivial born component to force double stabilization, showing the naive disconnected definition fails.
The disconnected birth component from the empty end is a degenerate move used to show the naive disconnected definition collapses toward smooth cobordism.
The disconnected birth from the empty component plus a pinch is a degenerate cobordism piece used to show the definition misbehaves without connectedness.
A disconnected join using an empty birth component is a degenerate enemy case showing why connected pieces are required.
The disconnected birth from the empty end plus pinch is a collapsed naive case used to show the definition misbehaves without connectedness.
The disconnected birth from the empty end followed by a pinch is a base-case/collapsing construction used to show the naive disconnected definition gives pathological double stabilizations.
The disconnected birth followed by a pinch is presented as a pathology showing that omitting connectedness collapses the relation toward smooth cobordism.
Presented as the bad nonconnected birth-then-pinch construction that would make double stabilizations equivalent if connectedness were omitted.
The disconnected birth of a trivial component followed by a pinch is a degenerate construction showing the definition would collapse by allowing double stabilizations.

The disconnected birth plus pinch construction uses an extraneous trivial born component to show that omitting connectedness would allow double stabilizations and collapse the relation toward smooth cobordism.
The disconnected birth-then-pinch shows the no-connectedness definition collapses by making double stabilization trivial, using an empty/disconnected birth component.
the disconnected birth plus pinch construction is a pathology showing why connected pieces must be required
